/// \returns the AA location that is being access by the instruction.
static MemoryLocation getLocation(Instruction *I) {

  if (LoadInst *LI = dyn_cast<LoadInst>(I))
    return MemoryLocation::get(LI);

/// \returns True if the instruction is not a volatile or atomic load/store.
static bool isSimple(Instruction *I) {
  if (LoadInst *LI = dyn_cast<LoadInst>(I))
    return LI->isSimple();
  if (StoreInst *SI = dyn_cast<StoreInst>(I))
    return SI->isSimple();
  if (MemIntrinsic *MI = dyn_cast<MemIntrinsic>(I))
    return !MI->isVolatile();
  return true;
}

static void addMask(SmallVectorImpl<int> &Mask, ArrayRef<int> SubMask,
                    bool ExtendingManyInputs = false) {
  if (SubMask.empty())
    return;

      (!ExtendingManyInputs || SubMask.size() > Mask.size() ||
       // Check if input scalars were extended to match the size of other node.
       (SubMask.size() == Mask.size() &&

      "SubMask with many inputs support must be larger than the mask.");
  if (Mask.empty()) {
    Mask.append(SubMask.begin(), SubMask.end());
    return;
  }
  SmallVector<int> NewMask(SubMask.size(), PoisonMaskElem);
  int TermValue = std::min(Mask.size(), SubMask.size());
  for (int I = 0, E = SubMask.size(); I < E; ++I) {
    if (SubMask[I] == PoisonMaskElem ||
        (!ExtendingManyInputs &&

      continue;
    NewMask[I] = Mask[SubMask[I]];

/// Order may have elements assigned special value (size) which is out of
/// bounds. Such indices only appear on places which correspond to undef values
/// (see canReuseExtract for details) and used in order to avoid undef values
/// have effect on operands ordering.
/// The first loop below simply finds all unused indices and then the next loop
/// nest assigns these indices for undef values positions.
/// As an example below Order has two undef positions and they have assigned
/// values 3 and 7 respectively:
/// before:  6 9 5 4 9 2 1 0
/// after:   6 3 5 4 7 2 1 0
static void fixupOrderingIndices(MutableArrayRef<unsigned> Order) {
  const unsigned Sz = Order.size();
  SmallBitVector UnusedIndices(Sz, /*t=*/true);
  SmallBitVector MaskedIndices(Sz);
  for (unsigned I = 0; I < Sz; ++I) {
    if (Order[I] < Sz)
      UnusedIndices.reset(Order[I]);
    else
      MaskedIndices.set(I);
  }
  if (MaskedIndices.none())
    return;
  assert(UnusedIndices.count() == MaskedIndices.count() &&
         "Non-synced masked/available indices.");
  int Idx = UnusedIndices.find_first();
  int MIdx = MaskedIndices.find_first();
  while (MIdx >= 0) {
    assert(Idx >= 0 && "Indices must be synced.");
    Order[MIdx] = Idx;
    Idx = UnusedIndices.find_next(Idx);
    MIdx = MaskedIndices.find_next(MIdx);
  }
}

/// \returns a bitset for selecting opcodes. false for Opcode0 and true for
/// Opcode1.
static SmallBitVector getAltInstrMask(ArrayRef<Value *> VL, unsigned Opcode0,
                                      unsigned Opcode1) {
  Type *ScalarTy = VL[0]->getType();
  unsigned ScalarTyNumElements = getNumElements(ScalarTy);
  SmallBitVector OpcodeMask(VL.size() * ScalarTyNumElements, false);
  for (unsigned Lane : seq<unsigned>(VL.size()))
    if (cast<Instruction>(VL[Lane])->getOpcode() == Opcode1)
      OpcodeMask.set(Lane * ScalarTyNumElements,
                     Lane * ScalarTyNumElements + ScalarTyNumElements);
  return OpcodeMask;
}

import re, pathlib, subprocess

p = pathlib.Path("llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp")

# 3) Fix truncated resizeToMatch signature inside ShuffleIRBuilder.
s = re.sub(r'^\s*void\s+resizeToMatch\s*\(\s*Value\s*\*&\s*V1\s*,\s*Value\s*\*&\s*\)\s*\{',

s = re.sub(r'^\s*void\s+resizeToMatch\s*\(\s*Value\s*\*&\s*V1\s*,\s*Value\s*\*&\s*$',
           r'    void resizeToMatch(Value *&V1, Value *&V2) {', s, flags=re.M)

# 4) In createShuffle(Value*,Value*,Mask), make dyn_cast robust to nulls.
start = s.find('Value *createShuffle(Value *V1, Value *V2, ArrayRef<int> Mask) {')
if start != -1:
    # Find function body end by brace matching.
    i = start + s[start:].find('{') + 1
    depth = 1
    end = i
    while end < len(s) and depth > 0:
        if s[end] == '{':
            depth += 1
        elif s[end] == '}':

        end += 1
    func = s[start:end]
    func = func.replace('Instruction *I1 = dyn_cast<Instruction>(V1);',

                        'Instruction *I2 = V2 ? dyn_cast<Instruction>(V2) : nullptr;')
    s = s[:start] + func + s[end:]

p.write_text(s, encoding="utf-8")

A conservative and targeted fix is to prevent pointer-typed shuffles from being added to GatherShuffleExtractSeq/CSEBlocks so they are not hoisted/CSE’d across blocks. I will update ShuffleIRBuilder to skip recording pointer-element shuffles in both createShuffleVector overloads and in resizeToMatch, then build and run the patch.

```bash
python3 - << 'PY'
import re, pathlib, subprocess

p = pathlib.Path("llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp")
s = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

def patch_block(after_create_line_pattern, instr_kind='Vec'):
    # Find the "Value *X = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(...);" and the subsequent
    # "if (auto *I = dyn_cast<Instruction>(X)) { ... }" block and wrap the insertion
    # of I into GatherShuffleExtractSeq/CSEBlocks with a guard that skips pointer vectors.
    pat = re.compile(
        r'(' + re.escape(after_create_line_pattern) + r'\s*'  # creation line
        r'(?:\n|\r\n)\s*'
        r'if\s*\(\s*auto\s*\*I\s*=\s*dyn_cast<Instruction>\(' + re.escape(instr_kind) + r'\)\)\s*\{\s*'
        r'(?P<body>[^}]*)\}', re.M)
    )
    def repl(m):
        body = m.group('body')
        guard = (
            f"if (!cast<VectorType>({instr_kind}->getType())->getElementType()->isPointerTy()) {{\n"
            f"{body}"
            f"}}"
        )
        return m.group(1) + "\n" + " " * 6 + guard + "\n      }"
    return pat.sub(repl, s)

# Patch the two createShuffleVector overloads and resizeToMatch helper shuffle.
s = patch_block("Value *Vec = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(V1, V2, Mask);", "Vec")
s = patch_block("Value *Vec = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(V1, Mask);", "Vec")
s = patch_block("Op = Builder.CreateShuffleVector(Op, IdentityMask);", "Op")

p.write_text(s, encoding="utf-8")

subprocess.run(["submit-patch"], check=False)
